title: Shopkeepers and owners - retail and wholesale
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations, overseeing multiple departments or locations. Duties and responsibilities include formulating policies, managing daily operations, and planning the use of materials and human resources, but are too diverse and general in nature to be classified in any one functional area of management or administration, such as personnel, purchasing, or administrative services. Usually manage through subordinate supervisors. Excludes First-Line Supervisors. Plan, direct, or coordinate activities of an organization or department that serves food and beverages. Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. (Based on 34 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Greet guests, escort them to their seats, and present them with menus and wine lists when necessary.
Monitor food preparation methods, portion sizes, and presentation of food to ensure quality standards are met.
Schedule and receive food and beverage deliveries, checking delivery contents to verify product quality and quantity.
Monitor budgets and payroll records, reviewing financial transactions to ensure authorized expenditures.
Schedule staff hours, assign duties, and assess staffing needs; recruit staff using methods such as advertisements or job fairs.
Establish standards for personnel performance and customer service, and resolve personnel problems.
Order and purchase food, liquor, wine, other beverages, equipment, and supplies.
Maintain food and equipment inventories, keeping accurate records and ensuring compliance with health and safety regulations.
Investigate and resolve complaints regarding food quality, service, or accommodations.
Monitor compliance with health and fire regulations regarding food preparation and serving.
Assess and ensure adherence to nutritional standards for dining establishments, considering factors like guest needs and costs.
Conduct menu planning, including food utilization based on anticipated guest numbers and preferences, and maintain records of items sold.
Perform food preparation or service tasks as necessary, and communicate with customers regarding products, services, and pricing.
Prepare financial reports to monitor budget and expenditures related to operations.
Develop and implement strategies to improve service, performance, and safety in the establishment.
Stock and maintain supplies, assisting with inventory management and organization.
Arranging for maintenance, repairs, and a variety of services needed for operation, including waste removal.
Assess the potential impact of climate change on business financial issues, such as damage repairs, insurance costs, or potential disruptions of daily activities.
Collaborate with product development teams to research, model, validate, or implement quantitative structured solutions for new or expanded markets.

Conduct in-depth investigations of suspicious financial activity, such as suspected money-laundering efforts, and evaluate business operations to identify risk areas for fraud, documenting all investigative activities and creating logs or databases of information about fraudulent activity.
Collect historical cost data to estimate costs for current or future products and analyze blueprints and other documentation to prepare time, cost, materials, and labor estimates.
Collect fees, commissions, or other payments according to contract terms and prepare written reports of investigation findings.
Develop tools to assess green technologies or green financial products, and propose sustainability initiatives based on cost effectiveness, technical feasibility, and acceptance.

Prepare promotional plans, sales literature, media kits, and sales contracts, and determine advertising medium to be used while consulting with company officials, sales departments, and advertising agencies.

Identify or investigate violations of natural resources, waste management, recycling, or other environmental policies, and develop reports or presentations to communicate the effectiveness of sustainability initiatives.

Plan, direct, or coordinate activities of an organization focused on the production, pricing, sales, or distribution of products while monitoring suppliers to ensure they efficiently provide needed goods or services within budgetary limits.
Prepare estimates for use in selecting vendors or subcontractors based on reviewing material and labor requirements, and consult with clients or vendors to discuss and formulate estimates.

Conduct special studies to develop and establish standard hour and related cost data or to reduce costs, and establish and implement departmental policies, goals, objectives, or procedures in conjunction with board members or organization officials.
Attend sales meetings, industry trade shows, and training seminars to gather information and promote products while keeping informed of industry trends and deals.
Prepare and maintain a directory of suppliers, contractors, and subcontractors, and set up cost monitoring and reporting systems and procedures.
Evaluate the risks and benefits involved in implementing green building technologies as related to promoting their products and services in the most effective way possible.
Implement or oversee environmental management or sustainability programs addressing issues such as recycling, conservation, or waste management.

Direct non-merchandising departments of businesses, such as advertising or purchasing and manage the movement of goods into and out of production facilities to ensure efficiency or sustainability of operations.

Conduct audits or interviews to evaluate potential clients and arrange meetings concerning issues involving their clients, as well as negotiate with managers, promoters, and other persons regarding clients' contractual rights and obligations.
Prepare periodic accounting statements for clients and hire trainers or coaches to advise clients on performance matters, such as training techniques or performance presentations.

Prepare cost and expenditure statements and other necessary documentation at regular intervals for the duration of the project and confer with engineers, architects, owners, contractors, and subcontractors on changes and adjustments to cost estimates.
Prepare and deliver sales presentations to new and existing customers to sell new advertising programs and to protect and increase existing advertising.
Establish pricing strategies for goods or services based on forecasts of customer demand and develop or implement product marketing strategies.
Identify new advertising markets and propose products to serve them, while explaining to customers how specific types of advertising will help promote their products or services.

Promote products online and manage marketing campaigns through various digital platforms including social media, e-mail marketing, and search engine optimization, while also creating promotional materials and maintaining online storefront.
Purchase merchandise and maintain optimal inventory levels, including ordering items and packaging sold products for shipping.
Hire, train, and evaluate personnel; assign tasks and work hours; monitor performance to ensure goals are met, and facilitate communication regarding duties and responsibilities.
Create and maintain customer account databases, correspond with customers regarding product inquiries, and address complaints to enhance customer service experience.
Develop business and marketing strategies, establish pricing policies according to profitability requirements, and prepare financial reports summarizing operational results.
Ensure that all operations comply with industry regulations and standards, conducting training sessions for employees and monitoring compliance systems to enhance operational effectiveness.
Conduct audits and investigations regarding compliance with policies and procedures, addressing issues that arise and documenting activities for review and improvement.
Analyze market trends and consumer behavior to identify opportunities for growth and recommend improvements in product offerings and marketing approaches.
Plan and organize activities for customers, engage in community outreach, and respond to emergency situations as needed.

Supervise wellness and fitness specialists, manage or oversee fitness or recreation facilities, ensuring safe and clean facilities and equipment, and organize and oversee fitness or wellness programs, including training sessions, events, or health screenings.
Write budgets and prepare or implement operational plans for recreational activities or programs to ensure proper resource allocation.

Conduct needs assessments or surveys to evaluate interest or satisfaction in wellness and fitness programs, and recommend or approve new program offerings to promote wellness and fitness and minimize costs.
Organize and oversee events, schedule guest appointments, and respond to customer inquiries or complaints to enhance customer service in wellness and fitness initiatives.
Develop or implement marketing strategies for wellness and fitness programs and sell related products or services, including memberships.
Review or evaluate proposals and bids, supervise staff or subcontractors, and maintain client databases to streamline operations and ensure compliance with safety regulations.
Participate in continuing education to maintain current knowledge of industry trends related to wellness and fitness.
Train staff in the use or sale of fitness programs and products, and ensure all staff understand job responsibilities and corporate policies, identifying ways to improve performance.
Maintain wellness and fitness-related records, reports, schedules, or compliance documentation, and track operational data to ensure quality oversight.
Provide technical support for wellness and fitness initiatives as needed and establish relationships to enhance customer engagement.
Identify project needs such as resources, staff, or finances by reviewing project objectives and schedules, and recruit or hire project personnel as necessary.
Identify, review, or select vendors or consultants to meet project needs, and negotiate with project stakeholders or suppliers to obtain resources or materials.
Monitor project milestones and deliverables, and request and review project updates to ensure deadlines are met.
Prepare and submit budget estimates, progress reports, or cost tracking reports, and report project status, such as budget, resources, technical issues, or customer satisfaction, to managers.
Monitor the performance of project team members to provide performance feedback, and propose, review, or approve modifications to project plans.
Schedule or facilitate project meetings, and produce and distribute project documents.
Submit project deliverables to clients, ensuring adherence to quality standards.
